name: songbird-collection
description: A custom marketplace of Claude Code plugins for both experienced developers and vibe coders, extending Claude Code with workflows, commands, and integrations that fit how you actually build.

plugins.name: forge
plugins.description: Investigates your codebase before you build. Parallel domain experts surface integration points and risks, an adversarial critic stress-tests the plan against real code — nothing gets implemented without your sign-off.

plugins.name: verity
plugins.description: Fetches current official Claude Code documentation on demand from code.claude.com — gives Claude truth-grounding from primary sources instead of training memory. Also provides a canonical reference for undocumented host MCP tools (spawn_task, mark_chapter, show_widget, and the full ccd_session and visualize families).

plugins.name: foreman
plugins.description: Prompt-engineering and project-roadmap plugin for Claude Code. /foreman:craft-prompt assembles self-contained, Anthropic-grade prompts via AskUserQuestion, carrying fixed truth-grounding and scope-discipline blocks plus any project-defined custom sections or section omissions from .foreman/config.json. /foreman:init bootstraps a project's ROADMAP.jsonl and Claude-suggestion policy. /foreman:roadmap picks the next task like a software architect and crafts its handoff prompt. /foreman:survey ground-truths the near-term candidates against the actual codebase, on demand, and persists findings back into the roadmap. A commit-triggered hook keeps roadmap status in sync and, opt-in, asks about newly-discovered opportunities (including already-built scope creep); a second hook blocks direct ROADMAP.jsonl edits, forcing changes through the CLI.

plugins.name: hush
plugins.description: Token-lean sessions at the harness level, not the prompt level. Ships a forced output style that rewires the system prompt itself — no preamble, no play-by-play, one outcome-first final message — plus a PostToolUse hook that mechanically shrinks Bash/PowerShell output before it enters context (ANSI/progress-bar strip, duplicate collapse, line caps; failures keep ~4x more, verbatim), and a dual-mode narration meter (PostToolUse + Stop) that injects one corrective line the moment mid-turn narration crosses budget — inside the offending turn, at most once per turn. Replaces prompt-injection style plugins: same terseness goal, stronger mechanism, and it also cuts the input-token side they never touch. Tune via HUSH_CAP_PASS, HUSH_CAP_FAIL, HUSH_NARRATION_BUDGET; disable with HUSH_DISABLE=1.

plugins.name: razor
plugins.description: YAGNI enforcement at the harness level, not the prompt level. Injects a compact first-rung-that-holds ladder (skip it / reuse it / stdlib / platform / installed dep / one line / minimum that works) into the main thread and — gated by agent type — into code-writing subagents only, so read-only Explore/Plan spawns never pay the injection tax. Backs the prompt with mechanical soft gates: a PreToolUse dependency guard that denies the first install of a new package with a reuse-first reason (the identical retry passes — one forced reconsideration, never a hard block), and a per-turn new-file meter that challenges file sprawl once, then self-clears. Boolean on/off (/razor off), no intensity dial. Pairs with hush: hush governs how the agent talks, razor governs what it builds. Tune via RAZOR_DISABLE, RAZOR_DEP_GUARD, RAZOR_FILE_BUDGET, RAZOR_AGENT_SKIP, RAZOR_AGENT_INJECT.
